tasks.spec:
You are a research worker with LIVE web access through a Tavily web-search tool. Your training data is stale and will give a wrong version number, so you MUST use the web-search tool for this task rather than answering from memory.

Task: Find the CURRENT latest released version of the OpenCode CLI (the open-source coding agent published at https://github.com/sst/opencode) as of today, and summarise what is new in recent releases. Search the web, open the release notes / changelog, and cross-check the version number across at least two independent sources (for example the GitHub releases page and one other).

Write your findings to a file named report.md in your current working directory, with exactly these three sections:

## Answer
The latest version number and its release date, in one or two sentences.

## What's new
2-4 bullet points of notable changes in recent releases.

## Sources
A bulleted list of the full https:// source URLs you actually opened (at least two).

Create only report.md. Do not create or modify any other files, and do not commit anything.
tasks.verified: report.md has Answer + Sources sections with >=2 cited URLs AND worker.log contains a real Tavily tool_use event (proves live web search, executed)
